recipes.title: Barley skyr strawberry walnut bowls
instructions: Simmer barley flakes with fortified soy milk for 6–7 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
Divide the porridge into three bowls, giving the husband the largest portion.
Spoon skyr over the warm barley and arrange strawberries on top.
Scatter walnuts and chia seeds over the bowls and pour kefir into a glass for the child.

recipes.title: Egg-white spinach tomato omelet with rye and cottage cheese
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wide non-stick pan and sauté spring onion and tomatoes for 3 minutes.
Add spinach and wilt for 1 minute, then spread evenly across the pan.
Whisk egg whites with whole eggs, pour over the vegetables, and cook on medium-low for 4–5 minutes until just set.
Toast rye bread and serve each plate with omelet, a generous spoon of cottage cheese, and kefir for the child.

recipes.title: Tofu mushroom rye scramble
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wide pan, add mushrooms and bell peppers, and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in crumbled tofu and turmeric and cook for 3 minutes, breaking it into curd-like pieces.
Add spinach, then pour in whisked eggs and egg whites and stir for 3–4 minutes until just set.
Toast rye bread and serve the scramble alongside it with fortified soy milk.

recipes.title: Buckwheat skyr orange walnut bowls
instructions: Simmer buckwheat groats with fortified soy milk for 10–12 minutes until tender and creamy.
Divide the warm buckwheat between three bowls, giving the husband the largest portion.
Top with skyr, orange segments, walnuts, and chia seeds.
Pour kefir into a glass for the child and serve while still warm.

recipes.title: Oat skyr raspberry flax walnut bowls
instructions: Simmer rolled oats with fortified soy milk for 4–5 minutes, stirring, until creamy.
Divide the porridge into three bowls, with the largest portion for the husband.
Spoon skyr over the warm oats and top with raspberries.
Scatter walnuts and ground flaxseed over each bowl and pour kefir for the child.

recipes.title: Cottage cheese oat banana pancakes
instructions: Whisk cottage cheese, eggs, egg whites, mashed bananas, oats, flaxseed, and baking powder until smooth.
Heat a non-stick pan with a thin film of olive oil over medium heat.
Cook small pancakes for 2 minutes per side until golden, working in batches.
Plate the pancakes, top with skyr, blueberries, and walnuts, and serve kefir for the child.

recipes.title: Smoked mackerel rye toast with scrambled eggs
instructions: Toast rye bread and arrange cucumber and tomato slices on plates.
Whisk eggs with egg whites and dill, then scramble in a non-stick pan with olive oil for 3 minutes until softly set.
Divide scrambled eggs and flaked smoked mackerel between plates, with the largest fish portion for the husband.
Serve the child's plate with extra cottage cheese and kefir alongside, and squeeze lemon over the fish.

recipes.title: Tuna white bean rye salad plates
instructions: Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, and mustard in a small bowl to make a dressing.
Layer greens, tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, beans, and tuna on three plates.
Drizzle dressing over each plate and scatter walnuts on top.
Serve with rye bread; pour kefir for the child to support calcium.

recipes.title: Lemon chicken chickpea quinoa traybake
instructions: Preheat the oven to 210°C. Toss chicken, chickpeas, zucchini, peppers, and red onion with olive oil, garlic, paprika, oregano, lemon zest and juice on a large baking tray.
Roast for 25 minutes, stirring once halfway, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are caramelized.
While the tray roasts, simmer quinoa in 640 ml lightly salted water for 12–14 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Divide quinoa between plates, top with traybake, scatter parsley, and serve with a spoon of Greek yogurt; reserve roughly half of the tray and quinoa, chilled, for Tuesday lunch.

recipes.title: Baked salmon with dill potatoes and green beans
instructions: Preheat the oven to 210°C. Toss potatoes with olive oil, half the dill, and garlic on a large tray and roast for 20 minutes.
Add green beans and leek, push to the sides, and lay salmon fillets in the centre topped with lemon slices.
Return to the oven for 14–16 minutes until salmon flakes and beans are tender.
Stir Greek yogurt with mustard and remaining dill; serve over the tray and reserve roughly half, chilled, for Wednesday lunch.

recipes.title: Tofu red lentil tomato spinach stew with brown rice
instructions: Cook brown rice in 720 ml lightly salted water for 28 minutes, then let stand 5 minutes off the heat.
While rice cooks, sauté onion, carrots, and garlic in olive oil for 5 minutes, then stir in cumin and paprika.
Add red lentils, tomatoes, and stock; simmer for 15 minutes until lentils are soft, then stir in tofu cubes and warm through.
Wilt spinach into the stew, finish with lemon juice, and serve over rice with a spoon of Greek yogurt; reserve half, chilled, for Thursday lunch.

recipes.title: Lean beef kidney bean barley vegetable pot
instructions: Brown beef in olive oil in a large pot for 5 minutes, then lift out and set aside.
Sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in the same pot for 5 minutes until soft.
Return beef to the pot with barley, tomatoes, stock, thyme, and bay leaves; simmer covered for 35–40 minutes until barley and beef are tender.
Stir in kidney beans and warm through for 3 minutes, finish with parsley, and serve with kefir for the child; reserve half, chilled, for Friday lunch.

recipes.title: Pan-seared mackerel with potatoes, beet salad and broccoli
instructions: Boil potatoes for 14 minutes until tender, drain, and toss with half the dill.
Steam broccoli for 4–5 minutes until just tender and bright green.
Pat mackerel dry, sear skin-side down in a hot pan with olive oil for 4 minutes, flip and cook 1 minute more.
Toss beetroot with red onion, vinegar, mustard, yogurt, and remaining dill; plate everything with lemon wedges and pour milk for the child.

recipes.title: Chickpea egg tahini bulgur bowls
instructions: Simmer bulgur in 560 ml water for 12 minutes, then fluff and let cool slightly.
Boil eggs for 8 minutes, cool under running water, peel, and halve.
Whisk tahini, yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, cumin, and 60 ml water into a creamy dressing.
Build bowls with bulgur, chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes, spinach, and parsley; top with halved eggs and dressing, drizzle olive oil over the husband's portion.

recipes.title: Turkey black bean wholegrain pasta bolognese
instructions: Sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in olive oil for 6 minutes until soft.
Add ground turkey and brown for 5 minutes, breaking it up, then stir in tomato paste, chopped tomatoes, oregano, and bay leaves.
Simmer the sauce for 15 minutes, then stir in black beans and spinach and warm through.
Cook pasta to al dente, drain, and toss with sauce; serve with Parmesan and milk for the child, reserving half of sauce and pasta, chilled, for Sunday lunch.

recipes.title: Cod chickpea olive tomato bulgur stew
instructions: Sauté onion, peppers, and garlic in olive oil for 6 minutes, then stir in paprika and cumin.
Add tomatoes, chickpeas, and olives; simmer for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile cook bulgur in 560 ml water for 12 minutes, then fluff with a fork.
Nestle cod into the stew, cover, and poach for 6–7 minutes until just opaque; stir in spinach to wilt, finish with parsley, and serve over bulgur with lemon and yogurt for the child.

recipes.title: Skyr berry walnut seed cups
instructions: Spoon skyr into three small cups, with the largest portion for the husband.
Layer berries over the skyr.
Scatter walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and flaxseed on top and serve.

recipes.title: Kefir cottage rye seed plate
instructions: Pour kefir into three glasses, with the largest portion for the husband.
Spoon cottage cheese onto plates with rye crispbreads and cucumber slices.
Scatter walnuts and pumpkin seeds across the plates and serve.

recipes.title: Soy yogurt walnut berry bowls
instructions: Divide soy yogurt between three bowls.
Top with raspberries and blueberries.
Scatter walnuts and flaxseed over the bowls and serve.

recipes.title: Hummus boiled egg vegetable walnut plate
instructions: Boil eggs for 8 minutes, cool under running water, peel, and halve.
Spoon hummus onto three plates and arrange vegetable sticks alongside.
Top each plate with a halved egg and scatter walnuts over the hummus; serve kefir for the child.

recipes.title: Greek yogurt orange walnut pumpkin cups
instructions: Spoon Greek yogurt into three cups, with the largest portion for the husband.
Top with orange segments.
Scatter walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and a pinch of cinnamon over each cup.

recipes.title: Cottage cheese banana cocoa walnut bowls
instructions: Spoon cottage cheese into three bowls, with the largest portion for the husband.
Top with banana slices.
Dust with cocoa powder, scatter walnuts and flaxseed, and serve.
